question_text: The module begins by explaining where God came from. It says God is not a product of creation but is the One who created creation. According to the text, what is true about God's relationship to everything that exists?
answer_options: Everything depends on Him, but He depends on nothing
He depends on creation to sustain His existence
He and creation came into being together
He created the world and then withdrew from it
correct_answer: Everything depends on Him, but He depends on nothing

question_text: The text teaches that the Bible reveals one true God who exists eternally as three distinct Persons. Which three Persons does it name as the Trinity?
answer_options: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit
The Father, the Son, and the angels
The Creator, the Word, and the prophets
The Father, the Spirit, and the Church
correct_answer: The Father, the Son, and the Holy Spirit

question_text: The module describes God as eternal, saying He has no beginning and no end. Based on this part of the text, what does being eternal mean about God?
answer_options: He does not age, weaken, or run out
He grows stronger over time
He had a beginning but will have no end
He exists only outside of time and cannot act in it
correct_answer: He does not age, weaken, or run out

question_text: When discussing who Jesus is, John 1 is used to make a clear point. The text says Jesus is not merely a prophet or teacher. How does the module describe Jesus?
answer_options: He is the eternal Word, who was God and through whom all things were made
He is the greatest of the prophets sent to teach
He is a force that guides believers into truth
He is a created being who later became divine
correct_answer: He is the eternal Word, who was God and through whom all things were made

question_text: The module corrects a common misunderstanding about the Holy Spirit. It states that in Acts 5 Peter says lying to the Holy Spirit is lying to God. What does the text conclude about the Holy Spirit?
answer_options: He is not a force; He is God, and He is personal
He is an impersonal power that God sends out
He is a created helper who serves God
He is only the influence of God on the heart
correct_answer: He is not a force; He is God, and He is personal

question_text: The text says when you receive Christ you are not just 'trying to be better.' Drawing on John 1:12-13, what does the module say happens to a person who receives Him?
answer_options: They are given the right to become children of God
They earn God's approval through better behavior
They are made into a force that guides others
They become prophets and teachers for God
correct_answer: They are given the right to become children of God

question_text: The module describes God's nature with several qualities meant to help you trust Him. It stresses that His justice is not cold and that He keeps His word and does not change. Which combination of qualities does the text use to describe God's nature?
answer_options: Loving, relational, absolutely faithful, and unchanging
Loving but distant and sometimes changing
Faithful only to those who first obey Him
Just but unconcerned with love or relationship
correct_answer: Loving, relational, absolutely faithful, and unchanging

question_text: The module asks why God couldn't just forgive sin. It compares God to a judge and explains the consequence of ignoring evil. According to the text, why is simply overlooking sin not an option for God?
answer_options: Because He is also just, and ignoring evil would make Him unjust
Because He is too distant to notice sin
Because forgiveness is not part of His nature
Because sin is too small for Him to care about
correct_answer: Because He is also just, and ignoring evil would make Him unjust

question_text: The text connects God's justice to the work of salvation, explaining that sin must be judged truthfully because justice is part of who God is. Based on this reasoning, how does the module describe what salvation actually is?
answer_options: God dealing with sin through Jesus, not God overlooking sin
God deciding to ignore sin out of love
God lowering His standard so sin no longer matters
God leaving people to fight sin on their own
correct_answer: God dealing with sin through Jesus, not God overlooking sin

question_text: The module teaches that the Trinity matters because God did not save us by accident. Pulling together what the text says about the Father, Son, and Spirit, why does it say the Trinity is important for salvation?
answer_options: Father, Son, and Spirit work together in one saving plan
Each Person offers a separate and competing path to God
Only the Son is involved in saving people
The three Persons take turns acting at different times
correct_answer: Father, Son, and Spirit work together in one saving plan
